# schema.md ‚ÄĒ Backtest Data Reference (v5.1)

> Authoritative schema for `backtest/Log.csv`, `backtest/Trades.csv`, and `backtest/TicData.csv`. Every analysis script reads this file before parsing. When the real data disagrees with this spec, **this file is updated first**, and the scripts are re-run against the corrected spec ‚ÄĒ never the other way around.

---

## 1. Log.csv ‚ÄĒ the main event log

**Format:** long-format CSV. One event per row. Row meaning depends on the `Tag` column.

**Generated by:** `ModularStrategy/StrategyLogger.cs`

### 1.1 Columns (22, in order)

| # | Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Timestamp | datetime | Bar close time. May be `0001-01-01 00:00:00` on some WARN / ZONE_MITIGATED rows (carry forward during parse). |
| 2 | Tag | string | Row type discriminator. See ¬ß1.2. |
| 3 | Bar | int | NinjaTrader bar index. `0` on lifecycle events that aren't bar-aligned (STOP_MOVE, ENTRY_FILL, T1_HIT, T2_HIT, TRADE_RESET, ZONE_MITIGATED, SR_ZONE_BROKEN WARN, TA_DECISION). |
| 4 | Direction | string | `Long` / `Short` / (empty). On exit ENTRY_FILLs, reflects the direction of the UNDERLYING trade (a Short trade's stop-exit still shows Direction=Short). |
| 5 | Source | string | Signal category (e.g. `ORB`, `SMF_Impulse`, `VWAP_Reclaim`, `Confluence`). |
| 6 | ConditionSetId | string | Specific logic version (e.g. `ORB_Value_v2`). On `TA_DECISION` rows this holds a trade_id-like label: `{ConditionSetId}:{yyyymmdd}:{bar}` (no HH_MM ‚ÄĒ differs from standard signal_id). |
| 7 | Score | int | Confluence score 0‚Äď100 (may exceed 100 on EVAL rows before capping). |
| 8 | Grade | string | `A+` / `A` / `B` / `C`. |
| 9 | Contracts | int | Position size. Populated on ORDER_LMT, ENTRY_FILL, TRADE_RESET. |
| 10 | EntryPrice | float | Intended limit entry on SIGNAL_* / ORDER_LMT; **actual fill price on ENTRY_FILL, including EXIT fills** ‚ÄĒ the column name is misleading (see ¬ß1.3 ENTRY_FILL). |
| 11 | StopPrice | float | Hard stop on signal rows; **new** stop level on STOP_MOVE; final stop on TRADE_RESET. |
| 12 | StopTicks | float | Entry ‚Üí Stop distance in ticks. |
| 13 | T1Price | float | Partial target on signal rows; T1 level on T1_HIT. |
| 14 | T2Price | float | Runner target on signal rows; T2 level on T2_HIT. |
| 15 | RRRatio | float | T1 distance / stop distance. |
| 16 | ExitPrice | float | 0 while open; populated on T1_HIT / T2_HIT. |
| 17 | PnL | float | 0 while open; realized on T1_HIT / T2_HIT. |
| 18 | SessionPnL | float | Cumulative session P&L at this event. |
| 19 | GateReason | string | Rejection reason on SIGNAL_REJECTED; format: `G<id>:<GateName>(<expression>)`. |
| 20 | Label | string | Multi-purpose. Human name on signal rows; `signal_id` on TOUCH_OUTCOME; **disambiguates ENTRY_FILL subtype** (`{signal_id}` / `Stop` / `T2` / `NoOvernight`); decision on TA_DECISION (`Hold`/`Exit`/`Tighten`); `BULL`/`BEAR` on ZONE_MITIGATED; empty on STOP_MOVE/T1_HIT/T2_HIT/TRADE_RESET. |
| 21 | Detail | string | Tag-dependent payload. See ¬ß1.3. |

### 1.2 Tag values (19 known types)

Grouped by role in the strategy lifecycle.

**System:**
| Tag | When |
|---|---|
| `SESSION` | Session open/close |

**Signal-generation funnel:**
| Tag | When | Key populated |
|---|---|---|
| `EVAL` | Every candidate signal evaluation | Score, direction, prices |
| `SIGNAL_ACCEPTED` | Signal passed all gates | Score, Grade, prices, Contracts |
| `SIGNAL_REJECTED` | Signal blocked | GateReason populated |

**Signal-time snapshots & forensics:**
| Tag | When |
|---|---|
| `TOUCH` | Signal-time deep market snapshot |
| `BAR_CONTEXT` | At signal time ‚ÄĒ 5 bars before |
| `BAR_FORWARD` | After 5 bars ‚ÄĒ 5 bars after |
| `TOUCH_OUTCOME` | After outcome window (includes :REJ: for counterfactuals) |

**Per-bar market state:**
| Tag | When |
|---|---|
| `FLOW` | Every bar |
| `STRUCT` | Every bar |

**Trade-lifecycle (post-acceptance execution):**
| Tag | When | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `ORDER_LMT` | Limit order placed after SIGNAL_ACCEPTED | Marks order submission |
| `ENTRY_FILL` | **ANY fill event (entry OR exit ‚ÄĒ name is misleading)** | Position opened (entry) or closed (exit); Label disambiguates. See ¬ß1.3. |
| `TA_DECISION` | Per-bar trade-mgmt reasoning while position open | Decision audit |
| `STOP_MOVE` | Stop level adjusted (BE arm / trail / MFE lock) | Order-management event |
| `T1_HIT` | Target 1 partial fill | Partial exit |
| `T2_HIT` | Target 2 runner fill | Runner exit (may be duplicated by ENTRY_FILL Label=T2 ‚ÄĒ see quirks) |
| `TRADE_RESET` | Position fully closed | Final trade state |

**Diagnostics:**
| Tag | When |
|---|---|
| `WARN` | Diagnostic events (sub-typed: RANK_WEAK, SR zone broken, ORB_VP_DIAG, ‚Ä¶) |
| `ZONE_MITIGATED` | Zone invalidated |

### 1.3 Detail-column parsing specifications

#### Context string (on EVAL, SIGNAL_ACCEPTED, SIGNAL_REJECTED)

```
h4=[+|-|0] h2=[+|-|0] h1=[+|-|0] smf=[bull|bear|neutral|flat]
str=<float> sw=<int>
```

#### GateReason (on SIGNAL_REJECTED only)

```
G<id>:<GateName>(<expression>)
e.g.  G3.5:ThinMarket(vt=150<400=0.40√ó1000)
```

Parse into `gate_id`, `gate_name`, `gate_expression`.

#### TOUCH_OUTCOME Detail

```
MFE=<f> MAE=<f> HIT_STOP=<0|1> HIT_TARGET=<0|1>
FIRST_HIT=<TARGET|STOP|NONE> SIM_PNL=<f>
BARS_TO_HIT=<i> CLOSE_END=<f> WINDOW_BARS=<i>
```

#### BAR_CONTEXT Detail (5 segments, `|`-separated, oldest first)

```
O:<f> H:<f> L:<f> C:<f> V:<int> D:<int> | O:... | O:... | O:... | O:...
```

#### BAR_FORWARD Detail (5 segments with timestamp)

```
T:<iso_ts> O:<f> H:<f> L:<f> C:<f> V:<int> D:<int> | T:<iso> O:... | ...
```

#### FLOW Detail

```
REGIME=<+1|0|-1> STR=<f>
| BD=<int> CD=<int> DSL=<int> DSH=<int>
| DEX=<+1|0|-1> BDIV=<0|1> BERDIV=<0|1>
| ABS=<f> SBULL=<int> SBEAR=<int>
| IZB=<0|1> IZS=<0|1>
| HASVOL=<0|1> SW=<int>
| TRD=<int> CH_L=<0|1> CH_S=<0|1> BOS_L=<0|1> BOS_S=<0|1>
```

#### STRUCT Detail

```
POC=<f> VAH=<f> VAL=<f>
| NEAR_SUP=<f>(<f>t) NEAR_RES=<f>(<f>t)
| H4=<high>/<low> H1=<high>/<low>
| PP=<f> SKEW=<f>
```

#### TOUCH Detail

```
ZONE_TYPE=<OB|FVG_BULL|FVG_BEAR|NONE> ZONE_LO=<f> ZONE_HI=<f>
| TRD=<+1|-1> BOS_L=<0|1>
| FVG_BL=<0|1> FVG_BL_LO=<f> FVG_BL_HI=<f>
| BD=<int> CD=<int> ABS=<f> SBULL=<int>
| POC=<f> VAH=<f>
| H4B=<f> BDIV=<0|1> REGIME=<+1|0|-1> ATR=<f> HASVOL=<0|1>
```

#### WARN Detail ‚ÄĒ sub-typed

```
RANK_WEAK [<module_id>] A=<int> B=<int> C=<int> D=<int>
                        Pen=<int> Net=<int> Mult=<float>

SR zone broken at <price> [<source>] close=<price>

ORB_VP_DIAG: bar accumulated levels=<int> vol=<int> time=<timestamp>
```

RANK_WEAK is the score breakdown. Layers: A (MTFA), B (S/R), C (OrderFlow), D (Structure). `Net = A + B + C + D ‚ąí Pen`.

#### SESSION Detail

```
OPEN  VWAP=<f> ATR=<f>
CLOSE VWAP=<f> ATR=<f>
```

#### ZONE_MITIGATED row

- Direction: `Long` or `Short`
- Label: `BULL` or `BEAR`
- Detail: `close=<f> zone=<lo>-<hi>`

---

#### ORDER_LMT Detail

```
LIMIT
```

Literal marker. All order parameters in structured columns.

Sample:
```
Direction=Short, Source=Confluence, ConditionSetId=HybridScalp_v1,
Score=74, Grade=B, Contracts=1,
EntryPrice=25670.00, StopPrice=25685.19, StopTicks=48.4,
T1Price=25666.25, T2Price=25645.68, RRRatio=1.17,
Detail="LIMIT"
```

Expected 1:1 with each `SIGNAL_ACCEPTED`.

---

#### ENTRY_FILL ‚ÄĒ ANY fill event (name is misleading)

**Critical reinterpretation as of v5.1:** `ENTRY_FILL` fires on every fill ‚ÄĒ both entries AND exits. The `Label` column disambiguates subtype.

| Label value                         | event_subtype | Meaning | Current count |
|-------------------------------------|---------------|---------|---|
| `{ConditionSetId}:{yyyymmdd}:{bar}` | `entry`       | Position opened | 795 |
| `Stop`                              | `stop_exit`   | Any stop triggered (original / BE / trail / MFE-lock) | 701 |
| `T2`                                | `t2_exit`     | T2 runner exit fill | 80 |
| `NoOvernight`                       | `forced_exit` | Session-end forced close | 12 |
| **Total**                           |               |         | **1,588** |

Detail column is typically empty.

**Structured columns for all subtypes:**

- `EntryPrice` = **actual fill price**. On exit fills, this is the exit price ‚ÄĒ the column name is misleading. Use this value regardless of subtype.
- `Contracts` = contracts filled in this event
- `Direction` = direction of the UNDERLYING trade (not the fill side). A Short trade's stop-exit shows Direction=Short.
- `Timestamp` = actual fill time
- `Label` = subtype discriminator (see table above)

**Parsing requirement:** during Phase 1 parsing, split ENTRY_FILL by event_subtype:

```python
entry_regex = r'^[A-Za-z_]+_v\d+:\d{8}(?:_\d{4})?:\d+$'

if label matches entry_regex:     event_subtype = 'entry'
elif label == 'Stop':             event_subtype = 'stop_exit'
elif label == 'T2':               event_subtype = 't2_exit'
elif label == 'NoOvernight':      event_subtype = 'forced_exit'
else:                             event_subtype = 'unknown'  # flag it
```

Never treat all ENTRY_FILL rows uniformly. Analysis scripts that compute slippage must filter to `event_subtype == 'entry'`. Scripts that compute realized P&L must use exit subtypes.

**Slippage computation (entries only):**

```
slippage_ticks = (ENTRY_FILL.EntryPrice - SIGNAL_ACCEPTED.EntryPrice)
                 √ó side_sign / tick_size
```

Where `side_sign = +1` for Long, `‚ąí1` for Short. Positive slippage means worse-than-intended fill (paid more for a long, sold lower on a short).

**Realized P&L per trade (from log alone ‚ÄĒ no Trades.csv needed):**

```
entry_fill_price = ENTRY_FILL(event_subtype=entry).EntryPrice
exit_fills       = [all ENTRY_FILL rows between this entry and next TRADE_RESET,
                    with event_subtype ‚ąą {stop_exit, t2_exit, forced_exit}]
               plus: T1_HIT rows in same range (ExitPrice is the partial price)

trade_pnl = sum over all exit events of:
            (exit_fill_price - entry_fill_price)
            √ó contracts_exited_in_this_event
            √ó side_sign
            √ó point_value
```

Where `point_value` comes from `INSTRUMENTS` dict in `strategy_config.py`.

**Caveat:** for trades that hit T1, `T1_HIT.ExitPrice` is the partial exit price. The runner's remaining contracts exit via a later ENTRY_FILL (usually `Stop`, sometimes `T2`). Both legs contribute to trade_pnl.

---

#### TA_DECISION Detail

Two-part structure: `<family>,<kvp_payload>` ‚ÄĒ split on first comma.

Sample:
```
Label: Hold
Detail: TA_GESTATION,sev=20 fam=Default act=Tighten reason=TA_TIGHTEN
  sev=45 thr=40/70 hard=1 adv=0 dir=20 ext=16 abs=0 stk=4 slopeScore=0
  pers=0 stage=5 bias=0 bpct=0.000 bd=0 cd=-168 slope=0.0 dsh=1 dsl=1
  buy=103 sell=53 absv=1.1 bull=0/0 bear=1/0 hist=1 t1=0 pft=-5.5 mfe=0.0
```

Parsing approach:
1. Split Detail on first comma ‚Üí `ta_family` (e.g. `TA_GESTATION`), rest ‚Üí kvp string.
2. Parse kvp as space-separated `key=value` pairs.

Known kvp fields (parse all; store unknown in `extra_kvp`):

| Field | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `sev` | Severity score (appears twice ‚ÄĒ outer and inner; take the inner/later value) |
| `fam` | Decision family (`Default`, others TBD) |
| `act` | Proposed action (`Tighten`, `Hold`, `Exit`, others TBD) |
| `reason` | Reason code (e.g. `TA_TIGHTEN`) |
| `thr` | Threshold pair `<lo>/<hi>` |
| `hard` | Hard-action flag (0/1) |
| `adv`, `dir`, `ext` | Severity components |
| `abs`, `stk` | Absorption, stack counts |
| `slopeScore`, `slope` | CVD slope score and raw slope |
| `pers`, `stage` | Persistence, stage markers |
| `bias`, `bpct` | Bias score, bias percentage |
| `bd`, `cd` | Bar delta, cumulative delta at this moment |
| `dsh`, `dsl` | Delta since high/low |
| `buy`, `sell`, `absv` | Volume breakdown |
| `bull`, `bear` | Directional counters `<num>/<denom>` |
| `hist` | History flag |
| `t1` | T1 hit flag (0/1) |
| `pft` | Profit in ticks at this decision point |
| `mfe` | MFE in ticks at this decision point |

**`Label` column** = the decision actually taken (`Hold`, `Tighten`, `Exit`, etc.). Distinct from `act` inside Detail which may be a *proposal* from one decision family.

**`ConditionSetId` column** = trade label in format `{ConditionSetId}:{yyyymmdd}:{bar}` (e.g. `HybridScalp_v1:20260104:334`).

‚ö†ÔłŹ **This differs from canonical signal_id** `{ConditionSetId}:{yyyymmdd_hhmm}:{bar}` ‚ÄĒ TA_DECISION omits `_hhmm`. Parsers must normalize. See ¬ß5.1b.

---

#### STOP_MOVE Detail

```
old=<f> afterT1=<True|False>
```

Sample:
```
StopPrice=25672.15, StopTicks=60.2, Detail="old=25687.19 afterT1=False"
```

- `StopPrice` = **new** stop price (post-move)
- `StopTicks` = new distance in ticks
- Detail `old` = previous stop price
- Detail `afterT1` = True ‚áí trailing a runner after T1 partial; False ‚áí BE-arm or pre-T1 move.

**Direction of the owning trade is not on this row** ‚ÄĒ look it up from the owning entry ENTRY_FILL within the trade's time range.

**Stop movement amount (signed)** = `(StopPrice ‚ąí old) √ó side_sign`, where `side_sign = +1` for Long, `‚ąí1` for Short.

---

#### T1_HIT Detail

```
remaining=<int>
```

Sample:
```
T1Price=25976.75, ExitPrice=25976.75, Detail="remaining=1"
```

- `T1Price` = T1 level
- `ExitPrice` = actual partial fill price
- `remaining` = contracts still open after partial (typically 1 for runner)

**A T1_HIT is followed by an exit ENTRY_FILL (Label=Stop or Label=T2) when the runner closes.** Both rows contribute to the trade's realized P&L.

---

#### T2_HIT Detail

```
full_exit
```

Literal marker.

Sample:
```
T2Price=26178.75, ExitPrice=26178.75, Detail="full_exit"
```

- `T2Price` = T2 level
- `ExitPrice` = actual runner fill price

‚ö†ÔłŹ **T2_HIT count does not match ENTRY_FILL(Label=T2) count** in current data (70 vs 80). See validation #16. Prefer `ENTRY_FILL(event_subtype=t2_exit)` as the authoritative fill source; `T2_HIT` is a secondary marker.

---

#### TRADE_RESET Detail

```
fill=<f> stop=<f> rem=<int> t1=<0|1> mfe=<f>
```

Sample:
```
Direction=Short, Source=Confluence, ConditionSetId=HybridScalp_v1,
Contracts=1, EntryPrice=25672.00, StopPrice=25671.75,
Detail="fill=25672.00 stop=25671.75 rem=1 t1=0 mfe=8.0"
```

- `fill` = entry fill price (duplicate of entry ENTRY_FILL.EntryPrice)
- `stop` = final stop position at reset time
- `rem` = contracts remaining at reset
- `t1` = did this trade hit T1 during its life? (0/1)
- `mfe` = max favorable excursion in ticks achieved

**Interpretation of `rem`:**

| `rem` | `t1` | Interpretation |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | 1 | Fully exited via T1 + T2 (or T1 + stop on runner) |
| 0 | 0 | Fully exited without T1 (full stop-out on entire position, or rare same-bar T2) |
| 1 | 0 | Position stopped out or cancelled before fill (never made progress) |
| 1 | 1 | Runner stopped out after T1 partial (**MFE-leak signature**) |

`Direction`, `Source`, `ConditionSetId` populated ‚ÄĒ use these to join to the owning SIGNAL_ACCEPTED.

### 1.4 Signal identifier format

```
canonical:  {ConditionSetId}:{YYYYMMDD_HHMM}:{BarIndex}
e.g.        HybridScalp_v1:20260104_2255:334

rejected:   {ConditionSetId}:REJ:{YYYYMMDD_HHMM}:{BarIndex}

TA_DECISION + entry ENTRY_FILL Label variant (missing HH_MM):
            {ConditionSetId}:{YYYYMMDD}:{BarIndex}
e.g.        HybridScalp_v1:20260104:334
```

Normalize both formats to `trade_id = {ConditionSetId}:{yyyymmdd}:{bar}` (drop HH_MM) during join.

### 1.5 The complete event funnel (v5.1)

```
SESSION OPEN
    ‚Ēā
    ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚Ėļ FLOW, STRUCT  (every bar, in parallel with everything below)
    ‚Ēā
    ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚Ėļ EVAL (candidate scored)
          ‚Ēā
          ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚Ėļ RANK_WEAK (WARN)                 ‚Üí silently dropped
          ‚Ēā
          ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚Ėļ SIGNAL_REJECTED                  ‚Üí gate vetoed
          ‚Ēā     ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚Ėļ TOUCH + BAR_CONTEXT + BAR_FORWARD + TOUCH_OUTCOME :REJ:
          ‚Ēā
          ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚Ėļ SIGNAL_ACCEPTED                  ‚Üí approved
                ‚Ēā
                ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚Ėļ TOUCH + BAR_CONTEXT + BAR_FORWARD + TOUCH_OUTCOME
                ‚Ēā
                ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚Ėļ ORDER_LMT                  ‚Üí limit placed
                      ‚Ēā
                      ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚Ėļ (cancelled, no fill) ‚Üí no follow-up
                      ‚Ēā
                      ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚Ėļ ENTRY_FILL(entry)    ‚Üí position opened
                            ‚Ēā
                            ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚Ėļ TA_DECISION √ó N       (per-bar reasoning)
                            ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚Ėļ STOP_MOVE √ó N         (BE arm, trail, lock)
                            ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚Ėļ T1_HIT                (0 or 1 times)
                            ‚Ēā
                            ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚Ėļ ENTRY_FILL(stop_exit) ‚Üź OR
                            ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚Ėļ ENTRY_FILL(t2_exit)   ‚Üź OR    ONE OF THESE
                            ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚Ėļ ENTRY_FILL(forced_exit) ‚Üź     closes the trade
                            ‚Ēā
                            ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚Ėļ T2_HIT                (paired with t2_exit fill)
                            ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚Ėļ TRADE_RESET           (final ‚ÄĒ marks trade closed)

SESSION CLOSE
```

### 1.6 Parsing quirks & conventions

| Quirk | Handling |
|---|---|
| Timestamp `0001-01-01 00:00:00` on WARN / ZONE_MITIGATED | Carry forward last valid timestamp. |
| `Bar=0` on STOP_MOVE / ENTRY_FILL / T1_HIT / T2_HIT / TRADE_RESET / TA_DECISION | Logger doesn't bar-align lifecycle events. Use `Timestamp` for ordering. |
| `NEAR_SUP=<price>(0.0t)` | Price is AT the level. |
| `POC=0.00 VAH=0.00 VAL=0.00` | Value area not yet accumulated ‚Üí NaN, not zero. |
| Multiple EVAL rows at same `(Bar, Source)` with different Direction | Legitimate for confluence modules; flag others. |
| RANK_WEAK ‚Üí EVAL association | RANK_WEAK WARN appears immediately after EVAL. Join by `[module_id]` + same `Bar`. |
| Theoretical max EVAL Score | `30 + 40 + 30 + 15 = 115`. Above = logger bug. |
| TA_DECISION `ConditionSetId` uses `:yyyymmdd:` not `:yyyymmdd_hhmm:` | Normalize during trade_id join. |
| `Bar=0` can mean either "not bar-indexed" or "actual bar 0 at session start" | Disambiguate via Tag + Timestamp proximity to SESSION OPEN. |
| `ENTRY_FILL` rows with `Label ‚ąą {Stop, T2, NoOvernight}` | These are **EXIT** fills, not entries. Split by `event_subtype` during parse. Never treat ENTRY_FILL uniformly. |
| `Count(ENTRY_FILL Label='T2') ‚Č† Count(T2_HIT)` | Current data: 80 vs 70. Logger inconsistency. For fill-price analysis prefer ENTRY_FILL(t2_exit); T2_HIT is secondary. Flag the 10-row discrepancy at Phase 0. |
| `NoOvernight` label | Session-end forced closes. Count per source ‚ÄĒ any source producing many is missing a time-based exit rule. |
| `Stop` label masks which stop triggered | Can't tell original vs BE vs trail vs MFE-lock from this row alone | Cross-reference STOP_MOVE history in the same trade's time range to find active stop at exit time. |
| ENTRY_FILL without preceding ORDER_LMT in the same session | Should be near zero among entry subtype. If >0, check for market orders bypassing limit logic. |
| SIGNAL_ACCEPTED + ORDER_LMT with no subsequent ENTRY_FILL(entry) | Legitimate `unfilled`. Mark as such. |
| TRADE_RESET carrying `t1=0` despite an earlier T1_HIT for the same trade | Logger bug; validation #12. |
| Trade spans multiple sessions (TRADE_RESET day ‚Č† entry day) | Flag in Phase 15; usually means session-end auto-close missing. |
| Multiple TA_DECISION rows per trade | Expected ‚ÄĒ one per bar while position open. |
| STOP_MOVE Direction column empty | Direction looked up from owning entry ENTRY_FILL within the trade's time range. |

---

## 2. Trades.csv ‚ÄĒ NinjaTrader trade executions

### 2.1a Log.csv is the primary execution source ‚ÄĒ Trades.csv is secondary

Log.csv emits `ORDER_LMT`, `ENTRY_FILL` (all fills ‚ÄĒ entries and exits), `STOP_MOVE`, `T1_HIT`, `T2_HIT`, `TRADE_RESET`, and per-bar `TA_DECISION` directly. These are authoritative for:

- **Slippage** (SIGNAL_ACCEPTED.EntryPrice vs entry ENTRY_FILL.EntryPrice)
- **Stop management** (STOP_MOVE before/after levels)
- **Partials / runners** (T1_HIT / T2_HIT / exit ENTRY_FILLs)
- **Realized P&L per trade** (entry fill price vs exit fill price)
- **Trade-management reasoning** (TA_DECISION audit log)

Trades.csv is used only to cross-validate that Log.csv fills match NinjaTrader's executed trade list.

Impact on phase plan:

- **Phase 3 slippage** ‚ÄĒ from Log.csv directly. Trades.csv join not required.
- **Phase 15 trade-mgmt diagnostic** ‚ÄĒ reads STOP_MOVE / TA_DECISION directly. Dramatic precision gain.
- **Phase 16 trade-mgmt optimization** ‚ÄĒ tick-level simulation retained, but the *baseline* comes from STOP_MOVE logs, not inferred stops.

### 2.1 Expected columns (placeholder ‚ÄĒ validated at Phase 0)

Check:
- Trade count matches Log's unique trades (entry-subtype ENTRY_FILL count, ¬Ī1 for open-position-at-end).
- Entry/exit prices match across sources within 1 tick.
- Any Trades.csv row without matching ENTRY_FILL = bug.

### 2.3 Row count, timestamp range, sample rows

*Filled at Phase 0.*

---

## 3. TicData.csv ‚ÄĒ tick-level market data

### 3.1 Columns (inferred from sample; validate at Phase 0)

| # | Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Timestamp | datetime | Millisecond precision |
| 2 | Tick ID | int | Sequential |
| 3 | ms_of_day | int | Milliseconds since session start |
| 4 | Price | float | Last traded price |
| 5 | Size | int | Trade size |
| 6 | Bid | float | Bid at tick time |
| 7 | Ask | float | Ask at tick time |
| 8 | Aggressor | string | `Buy` / `Sell` / `Unknown` |

### 3.2 Sample

```
2026-01-01 18:05:03.476,14,3476,25669.75,1,25669.50,25670.00,Unknown
2026-01-01 18:05:03.484,15,3484,25670.25,1,25670.00,25670.25,Buy
2026-01-01 18:05:06.308,22,6308,25668.75,1,25668.75,25669.00,Sell
```

### 3.3 Usage notes

With Log.csv now carrying all fills directly, TicData.csv's primary role shifts to:

- **Counterfactual simulation (Phase 16):** "what if stop had moved to X at time Y instead of the actual STOP_MOVE at Z?" ‚ÄĒ requires tick walk between entry and exit.
- **Slippage forensics:** verify the market actually traded through the intended limit price.
- **Gap detection:** tick absence during trading windows flags data-quality issues.

Use chunked reading. Cache per-trade windows to `Analysis/artifacts/tick_cache/{trade_id}.parquet`.

### 3.4 Row count, timestamp range

*Filled at Phase 0.*

---

## 4. Instrument parameters

From `Analysis/strategy_config.py`:

| Instrument | tick_size | tick_value | point_value | round_interval |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| MNQ | 0.25 | 0.50 | 2.00 | 100.0 |
| NQ | 0.25 | 5.00 | 20.00 | 100.0 |
| ES | 0.25 | 12.50 | 50.00 | 25.0 |
| MES | 0.25 | 1.25 | 5.00 | 25.0 |

`price_distance_in_ticks = price_distance / tick_size`.

---

## 5. JOIN STRATEGY

### 5.1 Within Log.csv ‚ÄĒ signal-generation stage

Primary key: `signal_id = {ConditionSetId}:{yyyymmdd_hhmm}:{Bar}`, `:REJ:` for rejected.

- `EVAL ‚ÜĒ RANK_WEAK`: same `Bar` AND `[module_id]` from WARN Detail equals EVAL's `ConditionSetId`. RANK_WEAK follows EVAL in log order.
- `EVAL ‚ÜĒ SIGNAL_*`: `(Bar, Source, ConditionSetId, Direction)`.
- `SIGNAL_* ‚ÜĒ TOUCH`: `(Bar, Source, ConditionSetId)`. Exactly one TOUCH per SIGNAL_*.
- `SIGNAL_* ‚ÜĒ BAR_CONTEXT / BAR_FORWARD`: nearest preceding/following with matching `Bar`.
- `SIGNAL_* ‚ÜĒ TOUCH_OUTCOME`: parse signal_id from `Label`.

### 5.1a Within Log.csv ‚ÄĒ trade-lifecycle stage

Canonical `trade_id` = normalized signal_id of the opening SIGNAL_ACCEPTED (see ¬ß5.1b for normalization).

Joins:

- `SIGNAL_ACCEPTED ‚ÜĒ ORDER_LMT`: `(Source, ConditionSetId, Direction)` + ORDER_LMT within 5 minutes after. 1:1 expected.
- `ORDER_LMT ‚ÜĒ ENTRY_FILL(entry)`: **entry ENTRY_FILL's Label contains signal_id directly** ‚ÄĒ parse it. Not every ORDER_LMT has an entry fill (unfilled limits).
- `ENTRY_FILL(entry) ‚ÜĒ TA_DECISION`: TA_DECISION's `ConditionSetId` carries the trade_id variant. Normalize (¬ß5.1b) and join.
- `ENTRY_FILL(entry) ‚ÜĒ STOP_MOVE / T1_HIT`: no ID on these rows. Match by chronological proximity ‚ÄĒ rows between the entry ENTRY_FILL and the next TRADE_RESET (in time order per instrument) belong to that trade. Direction inferred from the owning entry.
- `ENTRY_FILL(entry) ‚ÜĒ ENTRY_FILL(stop_exit / t2_exit / forced_exit)`: same proximity rule. Exit fills fall between the owning entry and the next TRADE_RESET. Typically one exit fill per trade (possibly preceded by a T1_HIT partial).
- `ENTRY_FILL(entry) ‚ÜĒ T2_HIT`: same proximity rule. T2_HIT is usually paired with an ENTRY_FILL(t2_exit) at the same or near timestamp.
- `ENTRY_FILL(entry) ‚ÜĒ TRADE_RESET`: chronologically next TRADE_RESET sharing `(Source, ConditionSetId, Direction)` after the entry.

### 5.1b trade_id normalization

```
TA_DECISION ConditionSetId:       HybridScalp_v1:20260104:334
entry ENTRY_FILL Label:           HybridScalp_v1:20260104:334
SIGNAL_ACCEPTED signal_id:        HybridScalp_v1:20260104_2255:334

Normalize:  regex replace  _\d{4}  ‚Üí  ""
Canonical trade_id:               HybridScalp_v1:20260104:334
```

Use the normalized form as `trade_id` throughout Parts 2 and 3.

### 5.2 Log.csv ‚Üí Trades.csv (sanity only)

Match by `(entry ENTRY_FILL.Timestamp, EntryPrice, Direction)`.

### 5.3 Log.csv ‚Üí TicData.csv

Time-window lookup. Per trade_id, extract ticks in `[entry ENTRY_FILL.Timestamp, TRADE_RESET.Timestamp]`.

---

## 6. Validation checklist

Every analysis script verifies:

1. Log.csv has 22 columns in ¬ß1.1 order.
2. `Tag ‚ąą {19 types in ¬ß1.2}`. Unknown Tag = spec drift, stop.
3. Detail-parse success ‚Č• 99% per Tag.
4. Every `SIGNAL_*` has exactly one `TOUCH`.
5. Every `EVAL` has either a `RANK_WEAK` or a `SIGNAL_*` follow-up.
6. `RANK_WEAK.Net == A + B + C + D ‚ąí Pen`.
7. EVAL Score ‚Č§ 115.
8. `Count(SIGNAL_ACCEPTED) == Count(ORDER_LMT) == Count(TRADE_RESET)` within ¬Ī1. Current data: 795 / 795 / 794. ‚úď

9. ENTRY_FILL subtype reconciliation:
   ```
   N_entries      = count(ENTRY_FILL where Label matches signal_id regex)
   N_stop_exits   = count(ENTRY_FILL where Label == 'Stop')
   N_t2_exits     = count(ENTRY_FILL where Label == 'T2')
   N_forced       = count(ENTRY_FILL where Label == 'NoOvernight')
   N_unknown      = count(ENTRY_FILL with label not matching any of above)
   N_total        = total ENTRY_FILL rows
   
   Assert: N_entries + N_stop_exits + N_t2_exits + N_forced + N_unknown == N_total
   Current:  795    +      701     +    80       +   12     +    0      == 1,588  ‚úď
   
   Assert: N_entries == Count(SIGNAL_ACCEPTED) within ¬Ī1
   Current: 795 == 795  ‚úď
   
   Assert: N_stop_exits + N_t2_exits + N_forced + N_unfilled_or_open ‚Čą N_entries
   Current: 701 + 80 + 12 = 793 vs 795 entries
            ‚Üí 2 missing exits ‚Üí 1 open at backtest end + 1 edge case.  Acceptable.
   
   Assert: N_unknown == 0. Any unknown label ‚áí new ENTRY_FILL subtype
   discovered; stop and update this schema.
   ```

10. Every `T1_HIT` and exit `ENTRY_FILL` falls between some entry `ENTRY_FILL` and the next `TRADE_RESET`. Orphans = trade-tracking bug.
11. Every `STOP_MOVE` falls between some entry `ENTRY_FILL` and next `TRADE_RESET`. Orphans = stray stop move.
12. For every `TRADE_RESET` with `t1=1`, a `T1_HIT` exists in the trade's time range. Mismatches = TRADE_RESET state bug.
13. `TRADE_RESET.Timestamp > entry ENTRY_FILL.Timestamp` always.
14. Every `TA_DECISION.ConditionSetId` (normalized) resolves to a known trade_id. Unresolvable = TA decision without open position.
15. RANK_WEAK join parse success ‚Č• 98% (Part 3 Phase 18).

16. T2 exit reconciliation:
    ```
    Assert: Count(ENTRY_FILL where Label='T2') == Count(T2_HIT)
    Current: 80 vs 70  ‚ÄĒ DISCREPANCY of 10 rows.
    
    Likely causes to investigate at Phase 0:
      - 10 trades emit ENTRY_FILL(Label=T2) but not T2_HIT Tag (logger gap)
      - 10 ENTRY_FILL(Label=T2) are orphans from cancelled orders
      - T2 logic fires ENTRY_FILL even when T2_HIT conditions not met
    
    Analysis workaround: prefer ENTRY_FILL(event_subtype=t2_exit) as the
    authoritative runner-exit source. Treat T2_HIT as a secondary marker
    that may be missing for ~12% of real T2 exits.
    ```

17. Realized P&L sanity:
    ```
    For each trade, compute trade_pnl = ő£ over exit events of
      (exit_price - entry_price) √ó contracts √ó side_sign √ó point_value
    
    Compare to sum of PnL column on T1_HIT / T2_HIT rows within the trade
    (if those rows populate PnL; T1_HIT PnL is partial pnl, T2_HIT is
    runner pnl).
    
    Discrepancies > $5 per trade = logger P&L calculation differs from
    log-reconstructed P&L. Use log-reconstructed as source of truth.
    ```

---

## 7. Version history

| Version | Change | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| v1 | Initial inference | Pre-spec discovery |
| v2 | Canonical 22-column spec, 6 Tags | StrategyLogger.cs shared |
| v3 | Added TOUCH, FLOW, STRUCT + microstructure | Deep-data revealed |
| v4 | Added EVAL, WARN, SESSION, ZONE_MITIGATED + FLOW/STRUCT extensions | Log sample revealed full funnel |
| v5 | Added 7 trade-lifecycle Tags (ORDER_LMT, ENTRY_FILL, TA_DECISION, STOP_MOVE, T1_HIT, T2_HIT, TRADE_RESET) with full Detail specs and trade_id normalization; shifted execution analysis from Trades.csv to Log.csv | Phase 0 output revealed 19 Tag types total |
| v5.1 | ENTRY_FILL is ANY fill event (entry + stop_exit + t2_exit + forced_exit), disambiguated by Label. Added event_subtype parsing requirement, realized P&L reconstruction formula, T2 reconciliation validation | ENTRY_FILL Label counts revealed 701 Stop + 80 T2 + 12 NoOvernight + 795 entries = 1588 total |
